member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Invariants
member.summary: Provides common constants and utility methods used across all FluentUnions source generators.
member.remarks: This class centralizes shared configuration and utilities to ensure consistency across all generated code.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Generators.Invariants.MaxElements
member.summary: The maximum number of elements supported in generated tuple overloads.
member.remarks: This value determines how many tuple elements are supported in generated extension methods. For example, with a value of 8, generators will create overloads for tuples with 2 to 8 elements. This aligns with C#'s built-in tuple support which provides special syntax for up to 8 elements.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Invariants.Tab(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an indentation string with the specified number of tab levels.
member.param: The number of indentation levels (each level is 4 spaces).
member.returns: A string containing the appropriate number of spaces for indentation.
member.remarks: This method ensures consistent indentation across all generated code, using 4 spaces per indentation level as per standard C# conventions.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.ActionExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates action-based extension methods for Option types with tuple values.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that allow executing side effects on Option values containing tuples. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, providing OnSome and OnEither methods for handling both cases.
Generated methods include: - OnSome: Executes an action when the Option has a value, with tuple destructuring - OnEither: Executes one action when the Option has a value, another when it's None
Example patterns:
OnSome<T1, T2>( this Option<(T1, T2)> option, Action<T1, T2> action)
OnEither<T1, T2>( this Option<(T1, T2)> option, Action<T1, T2> some, Action none)
These methods are useful for: - Logging or debugging Option values - Performing side effects without transforming the value - Chaining operations while maintaining the original Option
All methods return the original Option to enable fluent chaining.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.ActionExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.TaskActionExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates asynchronous action-based extension methods for Option types with tuple values using Task.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that enable asynchronous side effects on Option values containing tuples. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, supporting both synchronous and asynchronous actions on Task-wrapped Options.
Generated method patterns include:
OnSomeAsync overloads: 1. Async action on sync Option 2. Sync action on async Option 3. Async action on async Option
OnEitherAsync overloads: 1. Async actions on sync Option 2. Sync actions on async Option 3. Async actions on async Option
Example:
OnSomeAsync<T1, T2>( this Option<(T1, T2)> option, Func<T1, T2, Task> action)
All operations: - Return the original Option to enable fluent chaining - Use ConfigureAwait(false) for proper async context handling - Execute actions only when appropriate (Some/None cases)
These methods are useful for async logging, notifications, or other side effects while maintaining the functional pipeline.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.TaskActionExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.TaskBindExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates asynchronous Bind extension methods for Option types with tuple values using Task.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that enable asynchronous monadic binding (flatMap) operations on Option values containing tuples. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, supporting both synchronous and asynchronous binder functions on Task-wrapped Options.
Generated method patterns include: 1. Async binder on sync Option:
BindAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Option<(T1, T2)> source, Func<T1, T2, Task<Option<TTarget>>> binder)
2. Sync binder on async Option:
BindAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Task<Option<(T1, T2)>> source, Func<T1, T2, Option<TTarget>> binder)
3. Async binder on async Option:
BindAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Task<Option<(T1, T2)>> source, Func<T1, T2, Task<Option<TTarget>>> binder)
All operations: - Return Task.FromResult(None) immediately if the source is None - Use ConfigureAwait(false) for proper async context handling - Enable chaining of async operations that may fail or produce no value
This is the fundamental operation for async monadic composition with Option types.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.TaskBindExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.TaskFilterExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates asynchronous Filter extension methods for Option types with tuple values using Task.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that enable asynchronous filtering of Option values based on predicates. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, supporting both synchronous and asynchronous predicates on Task-wrapped Options.
Generated method patterns include: 1. Async predicate on sync Option:
FilterAsync<T1, T2>( this Option<(T1, T2)> option, Func<T1, T2, Task<bool>> predicate)
2. Sync predicate on async Option:
FilterAsync<T1, T2>( this Task<Option<(T1, T2)>> option, Func<T1, T2, bool> predicate)
3. Async predicate on async Option:
FilterAsync<T1, T2>( this Task<Option<(T1, T2)>> option, Func<T1, T2, Task<bool>> predicate)
All operations: - Return None immediately if the source is None - Apply the predicate only if the Option has a value - Return the original Option if predicate is true, None if false - Use ConfigureAwait(false) for proper async context handling
This enables conditional propagation in async workflows based on validation or business rules.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.TaskFilterExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.TaskMapExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates asynchronous Map extension methods for Option types with tuple values using Task.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that enable asynchronous transformations of Option values containing tuples. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, supporting both synchronous and asynchronous mapper functions on Task-wrapped Options.
Generated method patterns include: 1. Async mapper on sync Option:
MapAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Option<(T1, T2)> source, Func<T1, T2, Task<TTarget>> mapper)
2. Sync mapper on async Option:
MapAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Task<Option<(T1, T2)>> source, Func<T1, T2, TTarget> mapper)
3. Async mapper on async Option:
MapAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Task<Option<(T1, T2)>> source, Func<T1, T2, Task<TTarget>> mapper)
All operations: - Preserve None values without executing the mapper - Use ConfigureAwait(false) for proper async context handling - Return Task<Option<TTarget>> for composability
This enables functional composition in async workflows while maintaining Option semantics.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.TaskMapExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.TaskMatchExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates asynchronous Match extension methods for Option types with tuple values using Task.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that enable asynchronous pattern matching on Option types containing tuples. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, supporting both synchronous and asynchronous match functions on Task-wrapped Options.
Generated method patterns include: 1. Async match on sync Option:
MatchAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Option<(T1, T2)> option, Func<T1, T2, Task<TTarget>> some, Func<Task<TTarget>> none)
2. Sync match on async Option:
MatchAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Task<Option<(T1, T2)>> option, Func<T1, T2, TTarget> some, Func<TTarget> none)
3. Async match on async Option:
MatchAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Task<Option<(T1, T2)>> option, Func<T1, T2, Task<TTarget>> some, Func<Task<TTarget>> none)
All operations: - Ensure exhaustive handling of both Some and None cases - Use ConfigureAwait(false) for proper async context handling - Return Task<TTarget> for composability in async workflows
This provides a functional, type-safe way to handle Option values in async contexts.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.TaskMatchExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.ValueTaskActionExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates asynchronous action-based extension methods for Option types with tuple values using ValueTask.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that enable asynchronous side effects on Option values containing tuples using ValueTask for improved performance. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, supporting both synchronous and asynchronous actions on ValueTask-wrapped Options.
Generated method patterns include:
OnSomeAsync overloads: 1. Async action on sync Option 2. Sync action on async Option 3. Async action on async Option
OnEitherAsync overloads: 1. Async actions on sync Option 2. Sync actions on async Option 3. Async actions on async Option
Example:
OnSomeAsync<T1, T2>( this Option<(T1, T2)> option, Func<T1, T2, ValueTask> action)
All operations: - Return the original Option to enable fluent chaining - Use ConfigureAwait(false) for proper async context handling - Execute actions only when appropriate (Some/None cases)
ValueTask is particularly beneficial for side-effect operations that often complete synchronously, such as writing to an in-memory cache or performing validation.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.ValueTaskActionExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.ValueTaskBindExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates asynchronous Bind extension methods for Option types with tuple values using ValueTask.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that enable asynchronous monadic binding (flatMap) operations on Option values containing tuples using ValueTask for improved performance. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, supporting both synchronous and asynchronous binder functions on ValueTask-wrapped Options.
Generated method patterns include: 1. Async binder on sync Option:
BindAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Option<(T1, T2)> source, Func<T1, T2, ValueTask<Option<TTarget>>> binder)
2. Sync binder on async Option:
BindAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this ValueTask<Option<(T1, T2)>> source, Func<T1, T2, Option<TTarget>> binder)
3. Async binder on async Option:
BindAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this ValueTask<Option<(T1, T2)>> source, Func<T1, T2, ValueTask<Option<TTarget>>> binder)
All operations: - Return ValueTask.FromResult(None) immediately if the source is None - Use ConfigureAwait(false) for proper async context handling - Enable chaining of async operations that may fail or produce no value
ValueTask provides better performance than Task for operations that often complete synchronously, making it ideal for high-throughput scenarios.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.ValueTaskBindExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.ValueTaskFilterExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates asynchronous Filter extension methods for Option types with tuple values using ValueTask.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that enable asynchronous filtering of Option values based on predicates using ValueTask for improved performance. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, supporting both synchronous and asynchronous predicates on ValueTask-wrapped Options.
Generated method patterns include: 1. Async predicate on sync Option:
FilterAsync<T1, T2>( this Option<(T1, T2)> option, Func<T1, T2, ValueTask<bool>> predicate)
2. Sync predicate on async Option:
FilterAsync<T1, T2>( this ValueTask<Option<(T1, T2)>> option, Func<T1, T2, bool> predicate)
3. Async predicate on async Option:
FilterAsync<T1, T2>( this ValueTask<Option<(T1, T2)>> option, Func<T1, T2, ValueTask<bool>> predicate)
All operations: - Return None immediately if the source is None - Apply the predicate only if the Option has a value - Return the original Option if predicate is true, None if false - Use ConfigureAwait(false) for proper async context handling
ValueTask is ideal for filtering operations that typically complete synchronously, such as checking cached values or performing simple validations.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.ValueTaskFilterExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.ValueTaskMapExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates asynchronous Map extension methods for Option types with tuple values using ValueTask.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that enable asynchronous transformations of Option values containing tuples using ValueTask for improved performance in high-frequency scenarios. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, supporting both synchronous and asynchronous mapper functions on ValueTask-wrapped Options.
Generated method patterns include: 1. Async mapper on sync Option:
MapAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Option<(T1, T2)> source, Func<T1, T2, ValueTask<TTarget>> mapper)
2. Sync mapper on async Option:
MapAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this ValueTask<Option<(T1, T2)>> source, Func<T1, T2, TTarget> mapper)
3. Async mapper on async Option:
MapAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this ValueTask<Option<(T1, T2)>> source, Func<T1, T2, ValueTask<TTarget>> mapper)
All operations: - Preserve None values without executing the mapper - Use ConfigureAwait(false) for proper async context handling - Return ValueTask<Option<TTarget>> for optimal performance
ValueTask is preferred over Task when operations complete synchronously most of the time, reducing allocation overhead in performance-critical paths.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.ValueTaskMapExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.ValueTaskMatchExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates asynchronous Match extension methods for Option types with tuple values using ValueTask.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that enable asynchronous pattern matching on Option types containing tuples using ValueTask for improved performance. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, supporting both synchronous and asynchronous match functions on ValueTask-wrapped Options.
Generated method patterns include: 1. Async match on sync Option:
MatchAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Option<(T1, T2)> option, Func<T1, T2, ValueTask<TTarget>> some, Func<ValueTask<TTarget>> none)
2. Sync match on async Option:
MatchAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this ValueTask<Option<(T1, T2)>> option, Func<T1, T2, TTarget> some, Func<TTarget> none)
3. Async match on async Option:
MatchAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this ValueTask<Option<(T1, T2)>> option, Func<T1, T2, ValueTask<TTarget>> some, Func<ValueTask<TTarget>> none)
All operations: - Ensure exhaustive handling of both Some and None cases - Use ConfigureAwait(false) for proper async context handling - Return ValueTask<TTarget> for optimal performance
ValueTask provides superior performance for pattern matching operations that frequently complete synchronously, reducing allocation pressure in hot paths.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.ValueTaskMatchExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.BindExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates Bind extension methods for Option types with tuple values.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that enable monadic binding (flatMap) operations on Option values containing tuples. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, allowing developers to chain operations that may fail or produce no value.
Generated methods follow the pattern:
Bind<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Option<(T1, T2)> source, Func<T1, T2, Option<TTarget>> binder)
The Bind operation: - If the Option has a value (IsSome), applies the binder function to the tuple elements - If the Option is None, returns Option<TTarget>.None without executing the binder - The binder function itself returns an Option, enabling composition of operations that may fail
This is the fundamental operation for monadic composition with Option types.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.BindExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.FilterExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates Filter extension methods for Option types with tuple values.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that allow filtering Option values based on predicates. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, enabling developers to apply predicates to tuple values with automatic destructuring.
Generated methods follow the pattern:
Filter<T1, T2>( this Option<(T1, T2)> option, Func<T1, T2, bool> predicate)
The Filter operation: - If the Option is None, returns None without executing the predicate - If the Option has a value, applies the predicate to the destructured tuple elements - If the predicate returns true, returns the original Option - If the predicate returns false, returns None
This allows conditional propagation of Option values based on their content, useful for validation and filtering scenarios while maintaining Option semantics.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.FilterExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.MapExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates Map extension methods for Option types with tuple values.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that allow transformation of Option values when those values are tuples. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, enabling developers to map over tuple values with automatic destructuring.
Generated methods follow the pattern:
Map<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Option<(T1, T2)> source, Func<T1, T2, TTarget> mapper)
The Map operation: - If the Option has a value (IsSome), applies the mapper function to the tuple elements - If the Option is None, returns Option<TTarget>.None without executing the mapper
This enables functional composition while maintaining the Option's semantics.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.MapExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.MatchExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates Match extension methods for Option types with tuple values.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that allow pattern matching on Option types containing tuples. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, enabling developers to destructure tuple values directly in the Match method's some callback.
Generated methods follow the pattern:
Match<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Option<(T1, T2)> option, Func<T1, T2, TTarget> some, Func<TTarget> none)
The Match operation: - If the Option has a value (IsSome), applies the some function with destructured tuple elements - If the Option is empty (IsNone), executes the none function - Both branches must return the same type TTarget
This provides a functional, exhaustive way to handle Option values, ensuring both Some and None cases are explicitly handled while automatically destructuring tuples.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Options.MatchExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.ActionExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates side-effect action extension methods for Result types with tuple values.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that allow executing side effects on Result values without transforming them. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements.
Generated methods include:
OnSuccess - Executes an action only if the Result is successful:
OnSuccess<T1, T2>( this Result<(T1, T2)> result, Action<T1, T2> action)
OnEither - Executes different actions based on success or failure:
OnEither<T1, T2>( this Result<(T1, T2)> result, Action<T1, T2> success, Action<Error> failure)
Both methods return the original Result unchanged, making them useful for: - Logging - Debugging - Triggering side effects (like sending notifications) - Updating external state
These methods maintain the fluent chain while allowing observation of values.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.ActionExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.TaskMapExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates asynchronous Map extension methods for Result types with tuple values using Task.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that enable asynchronous transformations of successful Result values containing tuples. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, supporting both synchronous and asynchronous mapper functions on Task-wrapped Results.
Generated method patterns include: 1. Async mapper on sync Result:
MapAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Result<(T1, T2)> source, Func<T1, T2, Task<TTarget>> mapper)
2. Sync mapper on async Result:
MapAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Task<Result<(T1, T2)>> source, Func<T1, T2, TTarget> mapper)
3. Async mapper on async Result:
MapAsync<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Task<Result<(T1, T2)>> source, Func<T1, T2, Task<TTarget>> mapper)
All operations: - Propagate failure Results without executing the mapper - Use ConfigureAwait(false) for proper async context handling - Return Task<Result<TTarget>> for composability
This enables functional composition in async workflows while maintaining error handling semantics.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.TaskMapExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.BindAllAppendExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates BindAllAppend extension methods for Result types with error accumulation.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that combine existing Result values with new ones by appending values to form larger tuples, while accumulating all errors if any Results fail. It generates overloads for all combinations where the total tuple size is between 2 and MaxElements.
Generated methods follow the pattern:
BindAllAppend<TSource1, TSource2, TTarget>( this Result<(TSource1, TSource2)> result, in Result<TTarget> binder) returns Result<(TSource1, TSource2, TTarget)>
The BindAllAppend operation differs from BindAppend: - Takes a Result value directly instead of a function - Uses ErrorBuilder to accumulate ALL errors from both Results - If both Results fail, combines errors into a composite error - If any Result succeeds while others fail, returns the accumulated errors - If all Results succeed, combines all values into a larger tuple
This is useful for: - Validation scenarios where you want all errors reported - Combining pre-computed Results while preserving all error information - Building comprehensive error reports in data processing pipelines
The 'in' parameter modifier is used for performance optimization with value types.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.BindAllAppendExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.BindAllFactoryGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates BindAll factory methods for combining multiple Result values with error accumulation.
member.remarks:
This generator produces static factory methods on the Result struct that combine multiple Result values into a single Result, accumulating all errors if multiple failures occur. It generates overloads for combining 2 to MaxElements Results.
Generated methods follow the pattern:
Result.BindAll<T1, T2>( in Result<T1> result1, in Result<T2> result2) returns Result<(T1, T2)>
The BindAll factory method differs from Bind in that: - It accepts Result values directly (not functions returning Results) - It accumulates ALL errors from failed Results, not just the first one - Uses ErrorBuilder to combine multiple errors into a composite error - If all Results are successful, combines their values into a tuple Result
This is useful for: - Validation scenarios where you want to collect all validation errors - Combining pre-computed Result values - Scenarios where knowing all failures is more valuable than fail-fast behavior
The 'in' parameter modifier is used for performance optimization with value types.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.BindAllFactoryGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.BindAppendExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates BindAppend extension methods for Result types to accumulate values into tuples.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that combine existing Result values with new ones by appending the new values to form larger tuples. It generates overloads for all combinations where the total tuple size is between 2 and MaxElements.
Generated methods follow the pattern:
BindAppend<TSource1, TSource2, TTarget>( this Result<(TSource1, TSource2)> result, Func<TSource1, TSource2, Result<TTarget>> binder) returns Result<(TSource1, TSource2, TTarget)>
The BindAppend operation: - If the source Result is a failure, propagates the error - If successful, passes the values to the binder function - If the binder returns a failure, propagates that error - If the binder succeeds, combines all values into a larger tuple
This is useful for: - Building up a set of values through a series of operations - Accumulating results while maintaining error handling - Creating workflows that gather multiple pieces of data
Example usage:
Result<User> userResult = GetUser(id); Result<(User, Profile)> combined = userResult .BindAppend(user => GetProfile(user.ProfileId));

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.BindAppendExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.BindExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates Bind extension methods for Result types with tuple values.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that enable monadic composition of Result operations when working with tuple values. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements.
Generated methods follow the pattern:
Bind<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Result<(T1, T2)> source, Func<T1, T2, Result<TTarget>> binder)
The Bind operation (also known as FlatMap or SelectMany): - If the Result is successful, applies the binder function to produce a new Result - If the Result is a failure, propagates the error without executing the binder - Flattens the nested Result structure, avoiding Result<Result<T>>
This is essential for chaining operations that might fail, maintaining clean error propagation through a sequence of potentially failing operations.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.BindExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.BindFactoryGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates Bind factory methods for combining multiple Result-returning operations.
member.remarks:
This generator produces static factory methods on the Result struct that combine multiple Result-returning functions into a single Result containing a tuple of all values. It generates overloads for combining 2 to MaxElements operations.
Generated methods follow the pattern:
Result.Bind<T1, T2>( Func<Result<T1>> result1, Func<Result<T2>> result2) returns Result<(T1, T2)>
The Bind factory method: - Executes each function in sequence - If any function returns a failure, short-circuits and returns that failure - If all functions succeed, combines their values into a tuple Result
This is useful for: - Combining multiple independent operations that might fail - Short-circuit evaluation when any operation fails - Collecting results from multiple sources into a single Result
Note: Operations are executed sequentially, not in parallel.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.BindFactoryGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.EnsureExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates Ensure extension methods for Result types with tuple values.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that add validation to successful Result values. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, allowing post-success validation.
Generated methods follow the pattern:
Ensure<T1, T2>( this Result<(T1, T2)> result, Func<T1, T2, bool> predicate, Error error)
The Ensure operation: - If the Result is already a failure, propagates the error unchanged - If the Result is successful, applies the predicate to validate the values - If the predicate returns true, returns the original successful Result - If the predicate returns false, returns a failure Result with the provided error
This is useful for adding business rule validations after successful operations, maintaining the Result chain while ensuring values meet specific criteria.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.EnsureExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.MapExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates Map extension methods for Result types with tuple values.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that allow transformation of successful Result values when those values are tuples. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements elements, enabling developers to map over tuple values with automatic destructuring.
Generated methods follow the pattern:
Map<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Result<(T1, T2)> source, Func<T1, T2, TTarget> mapper)
The Map operation: - If the Result is successful, applies the mapper function to the tuple elements - If the Result is a failure, propagates the error without executing the mapper
This enables functional composition while maintaining error handling semantics.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.MapExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.MatchExtensionsGenerator
member.summary: Source generator that creates Match extension methods for Result types with tuple values.
member.remarks:
This generator produces extension methods that allow pattern matching on Result types containing tuples. It generates overloads for tuples with 2 to MaxElements (typically 8) elements, enabling developers to destructure tuple values directly in the Match method's success callback.
Generated methods follow the pattern:
Match<T1, T2, TTarget>( this Result<(T1, T2)> result, Func<T1, T2, TTarget> success, Func<Error, TTarget> failure)
This allows for cleaner code when working with Result types that contain multiple values as tuples, automatically destructuring the tuple in the success case.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Generators.Results.MatchExtensionsGenerator.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IncrementalGeneratorInitializationContext)
member.summary: Initializes the generator and registers the source generation logic.
